reference: I am fifty-six years old, weigh one hundred and thirty pounds, and have been having uncontrollable high blood pressure. I spent five days in the intensive care unit with a severe headache from nitroglycerin, which still did not bring my blood pressure down. It finally went down, and I was released. I have chest pains, and my blood pressure still runs high most of the time. If I have a heart catheterization and my blood pressure is high and cannot be brought down, will they perform the procedure or wait until they can manage it?
text: I am fifty-six years old, weigh one hundred and thirty pounds, and have been having uncontrollable high blood pressure. I spent five days in the intensive care unit with a severe headache from nitrobenzene, which still did not bring my blood pressure down. It finally went down, and I was released. I have chest pains, and my blood pressure still runs high most of the time. If I have a heart capitalization, and my blood pressure is high and cannot be brought down, will they perform the procedure, or wait until they can manage it?
